ogTitle: Milroy vs Milsons Passage: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Milroy and Milsons Passage.
heading: Milroy vs Milsons Passage — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Milroy, New South Wales and Milsons Passage, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Milroy has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Milsons Passage (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Milroy or Milsons Passage safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Milroy has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Milsons Passage scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Milroy scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Milsons Passage scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Milroy or Milsons Passage?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Milroy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
